help.overview: Video Director sets up smooth, repeatable camera motion for recording cinematic clips of the simulation: slow rotations, pans, and zooms at precise speeds, with the selected satellite's highlight dot optionally hidden so it does not appear in the footage.
help.imgAlt: The Video Director menu with rotate, pan, and zoom controls
help.imgCaption: Pick a motion direction and speed, then start the move.
help.howToUse:
1. Open Video Director from the bottom bar (Experimental mode).
  2. Set the Rotate Speed, Pan Speed, or Zoom Speed, and toggle the direction you want; changes apply immediately and opposite directions are mutually exclusive.
  3. Click Start Auto Rotate to begin the motion, and again to stop it.
  4. Enable Disable Selected Satellite Dot, Black Earth, or Milky Way for a clean cinematic backdrop.
help.tip1: Very small speed values give broadcast smooth motion; the defaults are tuned for slow, cinematic moves.
help.tip2: Combine a slow rotation with the time controls running at high speed for a classic orbit timelapse shot.
